perturbation.original_text: WebMD agrees to grant, and Emdeon agrees to accept, a license to use the Content (as defined below) in connection with various software products it has all right title and interest to (the "Software") in accordance with the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: WebMD agrees to grant, and Emdeon agrees to accept, a license to use the Data (as defined below) in connection with various software products it has all right title and interest to (the "Software") in accordance with the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "Content" to "Data" here introduces ambiguity. The agreement then refers to "Content" throughout, especially in Exhibit A and section 1. If "Data" is not explicitly defined to mean the same as "Content" as defined elsewhere, it creates uncertainty regarding the scope of the license. Emdeon might argue the license only applies to the specific data provided, not the broader "Content" as described in Exhibit A.
perturbation.contradicted_text: EXHIBIT A CONTENT TO BE PROVIDED WebMD shall provide Content as follows: The entire set of Anatomical Line Drawings. Anatomical Line Drawings are printable line drawings intended for physicians to draw on and illustrate diseases and conditions to patients. -2- Source: WEBMD HEALTH CORP., S-1/A, 9/8/2005
perturbation.justification: The change from "Content" to "Data" creates a direct contradiction with Exhibit A. Exhibit A explicitly defines "CONTENT TO BE PROVIDED" as "The entire set of Anatomical Line Drawings," creating a clear scope for what WebMD is obligated to provide. By replacing "Content" with "Data" in the license grant, the license's subject matter becomes ambiguous. If "Data" is interpreted differently from "Content," Emdeon could argue that the license does not extend to the Anatomical Line Drawings, thus directly contradicting the explicit content definition in Exhibit A, which specifies that WebMD shall provide Content in a certain form.

perturbation.original_text: If WebMD should reprint, issue a new edition, change or modify the edition, or otherwise enhance or edit the Content Emdeon has licensed (collectively, "Updates"), Emdeon has the option, at no additional cost, to receive the most recent Update.
perturbation.changed_text: If WebMD should reprint, issue a new edition, change or modify the edition, or otherwise enhance or edit the Content Emdeon has utilized (collectively, "Updates"), Emdeon has the right, at an extra charge, to receive the most recent Update.
perturbation.explanation: By replacing "licensed" with "utilized" and changing "option, at no additional cost" to "right, at an extra charge", a contradiction is introduced. The original text implies that updates are part of the initial license and are provided at no additional cost. The modified text implies that updates are a separate service that Emdeon must pay for. This contradicts the original intent of providing updates as part of the licensed content and directly impacts the financial obligations of Emdeon.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Payments. In consideration for provision of the Content and all rights granted hereunder, Emdeon hereby agreed to pay WebMD the amounts set forth on Exhibit B of this Agreement. All amounts payable shall be due be due thirty (30) days after the receipt of WebMD's invoice.
perturbation.justification: The modified text introduces a contradiction because it states that Emdeon now has the 'right, at an extra charge' to receive Updates, whereas the contradicted text states that 'In consideration for provision of the Content and all rights granted hereunder, Emdeon hereby agreed to pay WebMD the amounts set forth on Exhibit B'. The contradicted text implies a one-time payment for all rights, including updates, while the modified text suggests an additional fee for the updates, creating a direct financial contradiction and impacting the payment terms initially agreed upon. This conflicts with the fixed fee outlined in Exhibit B.

perturbation.original_text: All material notices and other communications required or permitted under this Agreement shall be in writing and notices will be deemed given three (3) days after they are sent by first class mail, return receipt requested, and one (1) day after they are sent by overnight courier or upon receipt if delivered by hand delivery, to the receiving party's address as set forth in the first paragraph above, or to such other address that the receiving party may have provided in writing to the other party for purposes of receiving notices as provided in this Section.
perturbation.changed_text: All significant notifications and other correspondence mandated or sanctioned under this Arrangement must be in textual format, with notifications regarded as served three (3) days post dispatch via standard postal service, confirmation of receipt sought, or one (1) day following dispatch through expedited courier service, or upon immediate acquisition via personal delivery, directed to the recipient's location as specified in the introductory paragraph, or to an alternative address duly communicated in documented form to the opposing party for the express intention of notification receipt, as delineated within this segment.
perturbation.explanation: Substituting the original language with convoluted synonyms (e.g., 'material notices' becomes 'significant notifications,' 'Agreement' becomes 'Arrangement,' 'in writing' becomes 'in textual format,' and so on) creates confusion and increases the potential for disputes. This introduced unclarity about whether or not the change is altering the way notice has to be delievered. This in-text contradiction now makes it difficult to establish a clear understanding of the obligations. The original version is clear, with little room for misinterpretation.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (b) Termination. In addition to expiration of this Agreement as set forth in Section 3(a) hereof in the event either party fails to perform any of its material obligations hereunder, the other party may terminate this Agreement at its election after thirty (30) days written notice to the party of such breach, if such breach is not cured by the party within such thirty (30) day period.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces convoluted synonyms and unclear language, especially regarding the definition of 'material notices' and the method of delivery. This creates confusion about whether the changes in language are also altering the way notice has to be delivered. This unclarity now introduces an in-text contradiction with the contradicted text, which references written notice for termination. The 'textual format' may be open to interpretation about whether it includes email, fax, or other methods, which contradicts the need for clearly delivered written notice as specified for termination in the contradicted text.
